contracts.title: Track P parity is legal baseline
Track X feature flags must not pollute Track P
Pixel analysis remains in System layer unless explicitly re-scoped
Blur-related SSOT must be singular and conflict-free
ScanViewModel must use PatchDisplayMap instead of local displaySnapshot dict
Blur haptic in ScanViewModel is removed; blur only gates frame rejection
Phase ordering is mandatory
EXACT-ONLY pipeline IO closed sets are inviolable
PresentLoop timewarp is pure reprojection with constant disocclusion fill
RFC3161 audit anchoring is client-first with start/end mandatory and 60s periodic anchors
Resource Wallet metering and quota charging algorithm is delivered in Phase 4 infrastructure
CostShield quota, rate-limit, and shadow-pricing policy kernel is deterministic Core logic in Phase 4
Auth, gateway rate limiting, idempotency, and runtime budget fuse are enforced server-side from Phase 7+
Watertight result gates measured volume
persist_chunk must verify expected_hash before durable write
Phase 0 golden fixtures must pass before any Track P migration
Track X feature flags default OFF
Feature flag namespace strategy must be consistent and mapped
Section numbering for §6.x remains monotonic in normative layers
Core forbids mutex/thread/atomic primitives
Single long-lived integration branch with phase tags
Each phase has deterministic Cursor runbook generated from SSOT
Mobile-first offload migration executes in governed batches and minimizes non-final cloud workload
Every migration batch must pass full gate sweep before the next batch starts
Cloud compute is reserved for final S5 optimization/render and legal archive closure
Phase 14 must enforce Tri/Tet runtime consistency and deterministic Kuhn-5 replay

constants.description: Frame-level rejection threshold for Laplacian variance
Guidance threshold target defined by latest V2.11 table
Maximum timewarp age before degraded fallback
GPU cost $/sec ($0.45/hr, vast.ai US RTX5090 on-demand median)
Single job cost kill threshold (3.2x max normal job $0.62)
Max GPU sec/user/day (3h = 8 jobs x 17min + 100% margin)
GCRA burst allowance (app cold-start sends ~7 requests)
Max chunk size bytes (5MB, = server MAX_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES)
Max parallel chunk uploads (iOS default connections/host=6)
Rate limit chunks/min (5 chunk/sec x 5MB = 200Mbps sustained ceiling)
EMA smoothing coefficient for display evidence [0.1-0.3]
Display growth multiplier for locked patches [1.2-2.0]
Local patch contribution to color evidence (Rule F)
Global display contribution to color evidence (Rule F)
Min gate quality for soft ledger write (B1 policy) [0.25-0.35]
S0 black threshold for grayscale mapping
Stale patch threshold in seconds (5 min)
View angle bucket size in degrees [10-30]
Minimum novelty to write to ledger
Huber loss delta for robust statistics
D-S conflict switch to Yager rule threshold
Default occupied mass for good observation
Default unknown mass for good observation
Default free mass for bad observation
Maximum load factor for open-addressing map
S5 minimum soft evidence for guidance
Display factor weight in border calc
Area factor weight in border calc
Stevens Power Law gamma for border brightness
Bevel radius as fraction of thickness
Min display delta to trigger flip
Haptic blur threshold (Laplacian variance; equals QualityThresholds.laplacianBlurThreshold)
Weight decay per frame for space carving
Min fraction valid depth pixels to accept frame
Maximum voxels updated per GPU frame
Hard safety cap on triangles per meshing cycle
Max CPU+GPU time for integration pass (ms)
Maximum voxel blocks across all resolutions
Maximum linear probe before giving up
Position delta threshold for teleport rejection (m)
Anchor shift threshold to mark blocks stale (m)
Every Nth integrated frame is keyframe candidate
Memory budget cap for retained keyframes
GPU fence timeout before frame skip (ms)
Camera distance from origin before recenter (m)
SDF update dead zone for fresh voxels (m)
Additional dead zone at max weight (m)
Max wall-clock time per meshing cycle (ms)
Lower clamp for MC zero-crossing t
Upper clamp for MC zero-crossing t
Min camera movement to trigger integration (m)
Min camera rotation to trigger integration (rad)
Min integration touches before mesh extraction
Fade-in duration after min observations met
Target meshing cycle time for congestion ctrl (ms)
Good cycle threshold for additive increase (ms)
Ceiling: per-device max blocks per cycle
Additive increase per good meshing cycle
Good cycles required before block count increase
Distance from block edge for normal averaging (m)
Translation speed above which meshing defers (m/s)
Angular speed above which meshing defers (rad/s)
Speed below which camera is idle (m/s)
Angular speed below which camera is idle (rad/s)
Laplacian multiplier for pro macro profile
Min samples before speed estimation reliable
Min bytes delta before progress update (64KB)
Min file size for chunked upload (5MB)
HDR max frame average light level (nits)
Max instance count per scene; §6.63.2 TAIS. Derived: 10-bit instance_id in TriangleSemanticBinding, 2^10=1024 covers indoor (typ. 50-200 objects) with 5× headroom.

Instance boundary split threshold; §6.63.2 TAIS triangle boundary detection. cos(36.87°)=0.8: MC triangle normal precision at TSDF 2cm resolution. Tighter >0.95 misses curved contacts; looser <0.7 false-splits planar surfaces.
Instance contact/coplanar distance (meters); §6.63.3 InstanceEdge CONTACT detection. = TSDF resolution (0.02m) / 2 = MC zero-crossing max error. Two surfaces closer than this are physically touching.

Max edges in InstanceGraph; §6.63.3. Worst-case: 1024 instances × d95=3 / 2 = 1536 undirected edges. 2048 = 33% headroom over worst case. Capacity limits must cover worst case, not average — exceeding silently drops edges and breaks scene graph. Memory: 2048×24B = 48KB (negligible).

Support relation detection threshold; §6.63.3 InstanceEdge SUPPORT type. cos(36.87°)=0.8: contact normal must be within 37° of gravity-up to qualify as support (object resting on surface). Same physical basis as K-INST-BOUNDARY-NORMAL-DOT.

Graph edge coplanar distance (meters); §6.63.3. Same derivation as K-INST-CONTACT-DISTANCE-M: half TSDF voxel = MC surface max error. Edges are added when two instance surfaces are within this distance.

Background cache alpha-dominance threshold; §6.63.6. Gaussian contributing >60% to pixel is primary occluder. Rejects 50/50 boundary splits (two overlapping Gaussians each at ~50% cause ambiguous occlusion) while capturing solid surfaces at grazing angles (~60-70% contribution). Higher >0.8 misses semi-transparent; lower <0.5 catches boundary bleeding.

Background cache min confidence [0-255]; §6.63.6. Midpoint of uint8 range. Observation must accumulate confidence ≥128 (via repeated observations) before being used for background recovery. Prevents single-frame noise from polluting cache.

Min observation count before background cache entry is eligible for recovery; §6.63.6. 3 = minimum for single-outlier rejection (2-of-3 voting). Independent of confidence threshold: even a high-confidence single observation could be a transient artifact.

§6.64.1 Observability: hard minimum median parallax floor (pixels). 3.0px is a robust matching floor under handheld motion blur/noise; final gate is dynamic via d_gate=max(K-OBS-MIN-BASELINE-PIXELS, K-OBS-REQ-PARALLAX-RATIO*d_req).

§6.64.1 Observability: dynamic parallax gate ratio. d_req=z*σ_d/σ_z_target from first principles, and d_gate=max(floor, ratio*d_req). 0.20 enforces at least 20% of theoretically required disparity to keep keyframe throughput while preserving measurable geometry.
§6.64.1 Observability: minimum keyframe overlap ratio. Below 0.70 feature matching degrades; above 0.85 baseline is insufficient. Sweet spot for indoor room-scale scanning.
§6.64.1 Observability: maximum keyframe overlap ratio. Above 0.85 means insufficient baseline for triangulation at target depth.

§6.64.1 Observability: target depth uncertainty at 3m (meters). Derivation: LiDAR achieves 1-2cm; pure vision target 1.5cm with multi-anchor fusion. This drives B_req(z)=z²σ_d/(f·σ_z_target). Tier 0: forensic-critical, changing this changes all downstream quality gates.
§6.64.1 Observability: minimum information gain per frame to consider it useful. info_gain = Σ_voxel max(0, 1/σ_new² - 1/σ_old²). Below threshold → prompt user to move to uncovered area.

§6.64.2 ScaleAnchor: minimum number of consistent anchor objects required to lock absolute scale. 3 anchors allows single-outlier rejection via median. Scale locked when std(estimates)/mean(estimates) < 0.05.

§6.64.2 ScaleAnchor: maximum coefficient of variation (std/mean) across anchor scale estimates for lock. 5% ensures sub-3cm accuracy at 3m distance. Derivation: door height 200±3cm (1.5% CV) + segmentation ±5px (0.5% CV) + focal ±0.1% → total ~1.6% CV; 5% gives 3× margin.
§6.64.2 ScaleAnchor: EMA smoothing factor for post-lock scale updates. 0.05 = 20-frame effective window. New anchor only accepted if within K-ANCHOR-OUTLIER-RATIO of current scale.
§6.64.2 ScaleAnchor: maximum relative deviation for post-lock anchor acceptance. New anchor with |scale_new - scale_current| / scale_current > 0.08 is discarded as misidentification.

§6.64.2 ScaleAnchor robust fusion: Huber transition threshold in sigma units for anchor residuals. Used in weighted M-estimation s*=argmin Σ w_i rho((s_i-s)/σ_i). 2.5σ suppresses semantic outliers without over-clipping valid anchors.

§6.64.3 Adaptive TSDF: truncation = k × σ_total. k=3.5 covers 99.95% confidence interval. Derivation: 3.5σ retains all physically plausible observations while rejecting outliers beyond 4σ. Actual truncation clamped to [τ_min, τ_max] voxels.
§6.64.3 Adaptive TSDF: minimum truncation distance in voxel units. Below 1.5 voxels, the zero-crossing detection becomes unreliable (MC needs at least 1 voxel on each side of surface).

§6.64.3 Adaptive TSDF: maximum truncation distance in voxel units. Beyond 6 voxels, TSDF integration becomes diffuse and surface localization degrades. Trade-off: larger τ helps with noisy depth; smaller τ gives sharper surfaces.

§6.64.3 Adaptive TSDF: maximum cumulative weight per voxel. Derivation: 2-3min scan, 30fps, each voxel seen ~80× avg, E[w_total]≈0.6 → W_optimal≈48; 64=next power-of-2, gives 33% headroom. Too high → old data dominates; too low → insufficient fusion.

§6.64.3 Adaptive TSDF robust kernel parameter. Observation weight uses Student-t kernel w_rho=(1+rho^2/nu)^(-(nu+1)/2). nu=4 gives heavy tails for pure-vision noise while preserving continuous monotonic decay.

§6.64.3 Adaptive TSDF: Mahalanobis distance threshold for ACCEPT→CAUTION transition. Pure vision noise is larger than LiDAR; 2.5 (vs original 2.0) prevents over-rejection of valid observations. Derivation: Bayesian posterior P(inlier|ρ=2.5)≈0.42 at 90% inlier rate.
§6.64.3 Adaptive TSDF: Mahalanobis distance threshold for REJECT. Pure vision: 5.0σ (vs original 4.0). Bayesian posterior at ρ=5.0: P(inlier)<0.001. Beyond this, observation is certainly noise.
§6.64.3 Dual-timescale: evidence EMA smoothing factor. β=0.93 → effective window ~14 observations. Stronger smoothing suppresses single-frame tier flapping (S3↔S2) while geometry remains full-rate.

§6.64.3: w_quality exponent for pure vision mode. LiDAR uses 2.0 (quadratic). Pure vision: 1.5 (compromise) because good frames are rarer without LiDAR stabilization; too aggressive weighting starves the system of data.

§6.64.4 FusedLoss: self-supervised depth consistency weight (pure vision). L_depth_self = |D_rendered(v_i) - warp(D_rendered(v_j), T_ij)| / σ_warp. Lower than LiDAR depth (0.5) because self-supervision has higher uncertainty.

§6.64.4 FusedLoss: normal consistency weight. Upgraded from 0.1→0.2. Surface quality is critical for pure vision (no LiDAR ground truth to fall back on). Only applied at S2+ evidence tier (low-evidence normals unreliable).

§6.64.4 FusedLoss: floater suppression weight (TIDI-GS inspired, 2026.01). L_floater = max(0, |D_gaussian - D_mono_nearest| - margin). Light penalty sufficient to suppress geometric outliers without disrupting valid thin structures.

§6.64.4 FusedLoss: Charbonnier loss ε. L_charb = sqrt((I-Î)²+ε²)-ε. Smoother than L1 at origin → optimizer-friendly. 0.001 chosen so transition from quadratic to linear behavior occurs at ~1/255 pixel intensity.

§6.64.5 Adam optimizer epsilon lower bound for adaptive epsilon schedule. eps_t=clamp(K-ADAM-EPSILON-SCALE*median(sqrt(v_hat)), K-ADAM-EPSILON, K-ADAM-EPSILON-MAX). Lower bound 1e-10 guarantees denominator stability.
§6.64.5 Adam adaptive epsilon upper bound. Prevents over-damping of updates in high-noise windows when median second-moment spikes.
§6.64.5 Adam adaptive epsilon scale factor multiplied by median(sqrt(v_hat)). 0.01 sets epsilon to ~1% of typical second-moment scale before clamp.

§6.64.5 Adam beta2. CHANGED from 0.999→0.99 for short training budgets (<20K iter). Time constant 100 steps (vs 1000). Faster convergence for minute-scale on-device training. If iter>20K, restore to 0.999.
§6.64.5 Adam warmup iterations. CHANGED from 500→300. Aligned with SH1 activation timing. 300 iters sufficient for lr ramp from 0.1× to 1.0×.
§6.64.5 DPC stall detection window. CHANGED from 500→300 iterations. +40% faster response to training plateaus. Triggers opacity reset when PSNR unchanged ±0.1dB for 300 consecutive iters.
§6.64.5 DPC periodic opacity reset interval. CHANGED from 3000→2500. +20% parasitic Gaussian cleanup efficiency.

§6.64.5 DC4GS-inspired angular coherence threshold for split. If mean(cos(angle(grad_i, grad_mean))) > 0.7 AND magnitude > thresh → SPLIT. Reduces primitives 30% vs magnitude-only (NeurIPS 2025 DC4GS).
§6.64.5 DC4GS angular coherence skip threshold. If coherence < 0.3 → gradient direction is noise, skip densification regardless of magnitude. Prevents wasted splits on transient gradient spikes.

§6.64.6 Trinity backward cosine alignment threshold (FP32 mode). Derivation: arccos(0.9999)≈0.81°; for 1000-dim gradient, random perturbation ~1/√1000≈0.032. 0.9999 is well above noise floor. Tier 0: forensic determinism requirement.
§6.64.6 Trinity backward cosine alignment threshold (FP16 mixed precision). Relaxed from 0.9999 due to larger FP16 quantization error. Still well above random (0.032).

§6.64.7 Audit: SHA-256 batch size. Accumulate 4 frames → single hash. Reduces SHA-256 invocations 4× while maintaining per-133ms audit granularity at 30fps. Hardware Secure Enclave amortizes startup overhead.

§6.64.7 Audit: RFC 3161 periodic anchor interval (seconds). Uniform policy for all sessions: mandatory anchor at session start + every 60s + mandatory anchor at session end. Client-first mode: mobile requests TSA directly; server primarily verifies and archives tokens.
§6.64.7 Audit: maximum allowed local-vs-TSA drift before flagging time_unanchored. 300s tightens temporal proof integrity while still tolerating intermittent network delay.

§6.64.8 Volume estimation: relative interval-width gate for Measured classification. Must satisfy ΔV <= max(K-VOLUME-INTERVAL-WIDTH-RATIO*V_mean, K-VOLUME-INTERVAL-WIDTH-ABS-M3). 2% keeps large-scene relative uncertainty bounded.

§6.64.8 Volume estimation: absolute interval-width floor (m^3) for Measured classification. Prevents tiny-volume cases from passing solely on relative ratio; 0.002m^3 (~2L) is strict but practical for handheld room/object scans.
§6.64.8 Volume estimation: minimum mesh closure ratio for Measured. 97% closure + unknown_voxel≤3% + connected_components≤2. Triple gate prevents single-metric gaming.
§6.64.8 Volume estimation: maximum unknown voxel ratio within bounding volume. Complements closure_ratio — a mesh can be topologically closed but have large unobserved regions inside.
§6.64.8 Volume estimation: maximum connected components for Measured volume. 1=single watertight; 2=allows room with one enclosed sub-object. >2 indicates fragmentation → downgrade to Assumed.
§6.64.9 Streaming: evidence weight in utility function U = w_e×evidence + w_v×view_prob + w_err×Δerror/bytes. 0.4 ensures S5 cells are prioritized over purely visual-impact cells.
§6.64.9 Streaming: view probability weight in utility function. Viewport-likely cells get second priority after evidence cells.
§6.64.9 Streaming: error-reduction-per-byte weight in utility function. Balances bandwidth efficiency against evidence and visibility priorities.
§6.64.9 Streaming: minimum S5 cell arrival ratio under constrained bandwidth (5-20Mbps). 99% of S5 cells must arrive before any non-evidence Layer 2 data. Gate test: low-bandwidth simulation.
§6.64.10 NoiseClassifier: gyro blur threshold (rad/s). CHANGED from 1.5→1.2. Pure vision is more sensitive to motion blur (no depth sensor fallback). Earlier detection → earlier burst trigger.

§6.64.10 NoiseClassifier: overexposure pixel ratio threshold. CHANGED from 0.05→0.03. Pure vision needs stricter exposure control (3% overexposed pixels → alarm). Clipped highlights destroy depth estimation from stereo.

§6.64.11 MC33 topology: maximum non-manifold edges. Hard gate: MUST be 0 for Measured classification. MC33/Extended MC33 with asymptotic decider guarantees manifold output. Tier 0: forensic mesh integrity.
§6.64.12 ExactOIT: tile OIT (S backend) K-buffer depth. 8 layers per 16×16 tile. Sufficient for 95%+ indoor pixels. Overflow cascades to U backend.
§6.64.12 ExactOIT: exact K-buffer (U backend) depth. 24 handles dense vegetation/transparent objects. Overflow cascades to X (A-buffer PPLL).
§6.64.12 ExactOIT: A-buffer (X backend) memory hard limit. 96MB prevents OOM on complex scenes. If exceeded, reduce throughput (skip frames) but never reduce OIT precision.
§6.64.4 CDGS confidence: SfM proximity σ in pixels. 8px radius → Gaussian decay. Pixels far from SfM points get low depth-loss confidence.
§6.64.4 CDGS confidence: mono depth gradient cutoff. |∇D|>0.5m/pixel → conf_mono=0. Prevents depth loss at depth discontinuities.
§6.64.4 CDGS confidence: minimum confidence floor. 0.01 prevents division by zero while allowing 1% residual regularization in zero-confidence areas.
§6.64.4 StableGS Dual Opacity: coupling weight |α_geo-α_app|. 0.02 is weak enough to not impede depth correction but strong enough to prevent permanent divergence.
§6.64.4 StableGS Dual Opacity: enable/disable. 1=dual path (α_geo+α_app); 0=single opacity fallback with TIDI-GS floater penalty.
§6.64.4 DropGaussian: per-iteration dropout rate. 10% = mild regularization for short training budgets. Prevents individual Gaussian overfitting. CVPR 2025 recommends 0.1-0.3.
§6.64.5 PocketGS: enable cached alpha-compositing + index-mapped gradient scattering for on-device backward. 0=disabled (Phase 2+ pending code availability).

§6.64.5 Micro-splatting: isotropy regularization weight. 0.005 penalizes only extreme needle-like Gaussians (condition number >50:1). Weaker than Micro-splatting's 0.01 because we have shorter training budget.
§6.64.5 FastGS MV consistency: relative depth error threshold for split validation. 2% = 6cm@3m. Below → skip (geometry converged); above → split confirmed.
§6.64.5 MH-3DGS: enable Metropolis-Hastings probabilistic densification. 0=disabled (DC4GS+MV as main path). Research mode only.
§6.64.5 MH-3DGS: initial sampling temperature. Higher→more exploration. 1.0 is standard starting point for NeurIPS 2025 MH framework.
§6.64.5 MH-3DGS: per-iteration temperature decay. 0.995→T halves every ~139 iter. For 20K budget: final T≈0.995^20000≈4.3e-44→fully greedy.
§6.64.5 MH-3DGS: proposal candidates per iteration. 128 = ~2ms overhead. Balances exploration breadth vs computational cost.
§6.64.11 DiffMC: enable differentiable marching cubes (WACV 2025 NeuManifold). 0=disabled, use MC33+post-check. Requires Metal/Vulkan porting from CUDA DISO package.
§6.64.12 Moment-Based OIT: enable MB3DGS unified backend. When ON, replaces S/U/X cascade with moment-based transmittance (no sorting). 0=use S/U/X cascade.
§6.64.12 Moment-Based OIT: statistical moment order. 4=optimal quality/cost tradeoff per MB3DGS (Dec 2025). 2=fast but artifacts; 6=better but +50% cost. Tier 1: affects rendering precision.

feature_flags.name: AETHER_FEATURE_FLAG_*
feature_flags.notes: Preferred namespace for Track X compile-time isolation

feature_flags.name: AETHER_FEATURE_FLAG_VISUAL_PRIORITY
feature_flags.notes: Aligned to Track X default-OFF contract

feature_flags.name: AETHER_FEATURE_FLAG_EDIT_VOLUME
feature_flags.notes: §6.63 AetherEditVolume: TAIS instance separation + dual-mode editing + incremental scene graph

feature_flags.name: AETHER_FEATURE_FLAG_PURE_VISION
feature_flags.notes: §6.64 AetherPureVision: 12-subsystem pure vision optimization — observability engine, scale anchor, adaptive TSDF, pure vision FusedLoss, optimizer/DPC, Trinity determinism, audit chain, volume estimation, streaming, noise classifier, MC33 topology, ExactOIT
